question:
Why is it generally considered poor social etiquette to give unsolicited advice?
A) It is illegal in most jurisdictions without a professional license.
B) It implies the recipient is incapable of managing their own life and hasn't asked for your input.
C) Advice is only valid when delivered in written form.
D) People are biologically unable to process information they didn't request.
